is_p_falciparum.evidence: The title and throughout the paper state the study focuses on 'Plasmodium falciparum'. The methods explicitly state 'Inclusion criteria were: monoinfection with P. falciparum'.

reports_target_markers.evidence: The paper reports Pfdhfr mutations at codons 51 (asparagine to isoleucine), 59 (cysteine to arginine), and 108 (serine to asparagine and serine to threonine). These are all curated target codon positions for Pfdhfr (51, 59, 108).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 provides explicit numerator and denominator counts for each DHFR mutation pattern. For example, '108-Asn only: 2 (8.0%)' in sensitive group (n=25), and specific counts for all mutation combinations across sensitive (n=25) and resistant (n=19) groups totaling 44 samples.

sub_country_location.evidence: The study reports collection from 'the village of Magoda, Tanga region, Tanzania' — a named village within a named sub-national administrative region (Tanga region), providing sub-country-level geographic specificity.

collection_window_within_3y.evidence: The paper states the study was conducted in 1995: 'In order to assess possible associations between in vivo test results and point mutations of the antifolate target genes, a second therapeutic study was undertaken in 1995 in the same area'. Sampling occurred within a single year (1995), well within the ≤3 year window.

african_field_samples.evidence: Samples are from infected Tanzanian children enrolled in a therapeutic field study: '44 children aged 1–10 years were enrolled in the study in the village of Magoda, Tanga region, Tanzania.' These are human field/clinical isolates collected in an African country (Tanzania), not lab strains or imported cases.

is_primary_study.evidence: This is a primary empirical study reporting original data from a therapeutic trial with PCR amplification and restriction enzyme digestion of parasite DNA. It includes Materials and Methods, Results with Table 1, and Discussion of findings. It is not a review, meta-analysis, commentary, or modelling paper.

duplicate_risk.evidence: This is a new field collection from a specific therapeutic study conducted in 1995 in the village of Magoda. While the paper references a prior 1994 study in the same area ('a second therapeutic study was undertaken in 1995 in the same area'), it explicitly states this is a second, separate study. The 1995 study enrolled different children (44 children) and was conducted the following year. No indication is provided that these samples are part of a multi-site consortium, named surveillance network, or previously published dataset. The samples appear to be unique to this study.

supplement.evidence: The paper provides complete frequency data in Table 1 with explicit numerator/denominator counts for all DHFR mutation patterns. The geographic location is specified at village level (Magoda, Tanga region). No supplementary data are referenced or required for extraction of the reported marker frequencies.

assessment.summary: This 1997 study from Tanzania investigates DHFR mutations in 44 P. falciparum isolates from Tanzanian children enrolled in a therapeutic trial with sulfadoxine/pyrimethamine. All 44 samples harboured mutations at one or more of three DHFR codons (51, 59, 108). The most common pattern (75% of samples) was the triple mutant 108-Asn + 51-Ile + 59-Arg. Table 1 provides extractable numerator/denominator frequencies for each mutation pattern stratified by clinical outcome (sensitive vs. resistant). Samples were collected in 1995 from Magoda village, Tanga region, Tanzania—a sub-country location. The study is a primary empirical field investigation.
